name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme

sections.details: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS) is implemented by Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India. It is a non-contributory pension scheme to provide social security to widows of poor family (BPL)  of the society.
sections.benefits: A pension of ₹ 300/- per month is provided to Widows between 40 years and 79 years.  For persons who are 80 years and above, the pension is ₹ 500/- per month.
sections.eligibility:
The eligibility criteria under Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS) is:

- The applicant must be a widow in the age group of 40-79 years.
- The applicant should belong to a household living below the poverty line according to the criteria prescribed by the Govt. of India
sections.exclusions:
Pension will be discontinued

- In case of remarriage of widow
- Once the widow moves above poverty line
sections.application:
### Application mode: Online

- One can download UMANG App or visit website [https://web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home](https://web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home) - The citizen can login using mobile number and OTP.
- Once logged In, citizen can search for NSAP.
- Click on “Apply Online”
- Fill the basic details, choose the mode of payment of pension, upload photo and click on “Submit”.

### Application mode: Offline

- Individuals can submit completely filled applications to the Gram Panchayat / Block Office in the rural area and the Municipality / Municipal Council in the urban area, as per eligibility.
- A Verification Officer or Verification Team under an authorized officer verifies the applications with reference to facts related to eligibility
- The Verification Officer make the necessary recommendation for sanction or rejection with reasons.
- The list of applicants with the recommendations of the Verifying Authority is discussed in the Gram Sabha in rural areas or Ward Sabha / Area Sabha as designated by the State Government in urban areas and thereafter in the Gram Panchayats and Municipalities.
- If the time limits are not adhered to by Gram Sabha / Ward Sabhas, Gram Panchayats / Municipalities the Verification Officer directly submits his recommendations to the Sanctioning Authority under intimation to the Gram Panchayat / Municipality.
- After receipt of applications which are verified and recommended by the Gram Sabha / Ward Committee / Area Sabha, the Sanctioning Authority convey approval to the applicant in the form of a Sanction Order with a copy to Gram Panchayat / Municipality concerned.
- The Sanctioning Authority issues Sanction Order under his seal
- Every beneficiary who has been sanctioned pension under schemes of NSAP are issued a Pension Passbook. The Passbook contain details of the Sanction Order, particulars of the pensioner and disbursement details
- The list of beneficiaries to whom sanctions are issued is displayed at the Gram Panchayat / Ward / Municipal Office and updated every three months.
- The Pension amount is directly paid to the beneficiaries through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) process to their post office or bank account.
sections.faq:
**Q: What are different modes of receiving Pension?**

A: One can receive pension amount in Bank Account, Post Office Account, Postal Money Order or in hand cash.

**Q: Who will disburse in hand cash?**

A: In hand cash will be disbursed by Pension Disbursement Authority of respective Sub District/Municipal Area. One can select the pension disbursement authority while filling online application form on UMANG.

**Q: What pension amount is provided under the scheme?**

A: Eligible beneficiaries receive Central Assistance of ₹300 per month. Beneficiaries aged 80 years and above receive ₹500 per month.

**Q: Is there any income criterion for availing the scheme?**

A: Yes. The applicant must belong to a household identified as living Below Poverty Line (BPL) as per the criteria prescribed by the Government of India.

**Q: Can a widow above 80 years continue receiving benefits?**

A: Yes. Upon attaining 80 years of age, the beneficiary becomes eligible for enhanced assistance of ₹500 per month.

**Q: How can an eligible widow apply for the pension?**

A: Applications can be submitted through the prescribed process followed by the concerned State Government, Gram Panchayat, Municipality, or designated welfare authority.

**Q: How is the pension amount disbursed to beneficiaries?**

A: The pension is generally transferred directly to the beneficiary's bank or post office account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

**Q: Can a widow receive benefits from both Central and State pension schemes?**

A: States may provide additional assistance over and above the Central contribution, subject to their respective rules and policies.

**Q: What is the objective of the Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme?**

A: The scheme aims to provide financial security and social assistance to destitute widows from economically vulnerable households, helping them meet their basic needs and live with dignity.
